original_query: select  min(i_item_id)  ,min(i_item_desc)  ,min(s_store_id)  ,min(s_store_name)  ,min(ss_net_profit)  ,min(sr_net_loss)  ,min(cs_net_profit)  ,min(ss_item_sk)  ,min(sr_ticket_number)  ,min(cs_order_number)  from  store_sales  ,store_returns  ,catalog_sales  ,date_dim d1  ,date_dim d2  ,date_dim d3  ,store  ,item  where  d1.d_moy = 9  and d1.d_year = 1999  and d1.d_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk  and i_item_sk = ss_item_sk  and s_store_sk = ss_store_sk  and ss_customer_sk = sr_customer_sk  and ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk  and ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number  and sr_returned_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk  and d2.d_moy               between 9 and  9 + 2  and d2.d_year              = 1999  and sr_customer_sk = cs_bill_customer_sk  and sr_item_sk = cs_item_sk  and cs_sold_date_sk = d3.d_date_sk  and d3.d_moy               between 9 and  9 + 2  and d3.d_year              = 1999  ;
rewritten_query:
WITH date_sept_1999 AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_moy = 9 AND d_year = 1999
),
date_sept_nov_1999 AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_moy BETWEEN 9 AND 11 AND d_year = 1999
),
filtered_store_sales AS (
    SELECT ss_customer_sk, ss_item_sk, ss_ticket_number, ss_store_sk, ss_net_profit
    FROM store_sales
    WHERE ss_sold_date_sk IN (SELECT d_date_sk FROM date_sept_1999)
),
filtered_store_returns AS (
    SELECT sr_customer_sk, sr_item_sk, sr_ticket_number, sr_net_loss
    FROM store_returns
    WHERE sr_returned_date_sk IN (SELECT d_date_sk FROM date_sept_nov_1999)
),
filtered_catalog_sales AS (
    SELECT cs_bill_customer_sk, cs_item_sk, cs_order_number, cs_net_profit
    FROM catalog_sales
    WHERE cs_sold_date_sk IN (SELECT d_date_sk FROM date_sept_nov_1999)
)
SELECT 
    MIN(i_item_id),
    MIN(i_item_desc),
    MIN(s_store_id),
    MIN(s_store_name),
    MIN(ss.ss_net_profit),
    MIN(sr.sr_net_loss),
    MIN(cs.cs_net_profit),
    MIN(ss.ss_item_sk),
    MIN(sr.sr_ticket_number),
    MIN(cs.cs_order_number)
FROM 
    filtered_store_sales ss
    JOIN filtered_store_returns sr ON ss.ss_customer_sk = sr.sr_customer_sk 
                                   AND ss.ss_item_sk = sr.sr_item_sk 
                                   AND ss.ss_ticket_number = sr.sr_ticket_number
    JOIN filtered_catalog_sales cs ON sr.sr_customer_sk = cs.cs_bill_customer_sk 
                                   AND sr.sr_item_sk = cs.cs_item_sk
    JOIN store s ON ss.ss_store_sk = s.s_store_sk
    JOIN item i ON ss.ss_item_sk = i.i_item_sk;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: No specific predicate simplification was suggested in the analysis.

The analysis suggests the query is already optimized given the current schema and indexes. The high cost is due to the inherent size of the data involved, but there's no obvious rewrite that would significantly improve performance.
